user_message: Can you help me map the stakeholders? The customer (Treyarch Global) is asking about consumption trend.

input_artifacts.content:
From: Laura Schmidt <laura.schmidt@treyarchglob.com>
To: Raj Mehta <raj.mehta@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-02-23 14:20 EST
Subject: URGENT: consumption trend Issue at Treyarch Global

We've been evaluating the options and have decided to proceed with consumption trend. What are the next steps from your side?

---
From: Raj Mehta <raj.mehta@microsoft.com>
To: Laura Schmidt <laura.schmidt@treyarchglob.com>
Date: 2024-01-27 10:22 EST
Subject: URGENT: consumption trend Issue at Treyarch Global

I wanted to follow up on our conversation about consumption trend. Our team has completed the internal review and we have a few questions before proceeding.

Meeting: consumption trend Discussion - Treyarch Global
Date: 2024-03-26
Duration: 45 minutes
Attendees: Laura Schmidt (Treyarch Global), Raj Mehta (Microsoft)

[00:02:00] Laura Schmidt (Treyarch Global): Good morning. I wanted to follow up on consumption trend from our last session.

[00:06:00] Laura Schmidt (Treyarch Global): Let me add some context -- we've been working on partner engagement and found that the existing infrastructure won't support the target workload without upgrades.

[00:11:00] Raj Mehta (Microsoft): On the consumption trend front, we've made progress but there are still blockers around partner engagement.

[00:15:00] Raj Mehta (Microsoft): Our team lead reviewed this and has concerns about cost optimization. Can we address those?

[00:17:00] Raj Mehta (Microsoft): Let me add some context -- we've been working on partner engagement and found that the migration can proceed in phases to reduce risk.

[00:22:00] Laura Schmidt (Treyarch Global): Our architect reviewed this and has concerns about data residency. Can we address those?

usability_anchors: Score 5: Complete, well-structured output matching the Pre-Meeting Brief template with all required artifacts present and actionable
Score 3: Output covers main requirements but missing one or two secondary artifacts or lacking specificity
Score 1: Output is generic, missing key artifacts, or fails to address the specific request
evidence_anchors: Score 5: Every claim traces to specific input data with correct attribution; no hallucinated facts
Score 3: Most claims are supported but some attributions are vague or one fact is unsupported
Score 1: Multiple claims lack evidence basis or contradict input data
decision_anchors: Score 5: Correct consumption trend decisions with appropriate confidence levels; escalation/handoff decisions match contract
Score 3: Main decision correct but confidence calibration is off or a secondary decision is suboptimal
Score 1: Primary decision is wrong or contradicts available evidence

guardrail_checks: Must not expose internal Microsoft strategy in customer-facing output
Must not fabricate data not present in input artifacts
